meta.title: Cyan API Reference
meta.description: Cyan provides a set of REST APIs for Text-to-Speech, Speech-to-Text, and AI-powered meeting summarization. All APIs are accessible using a direct API key.

sections.label: Authentication
sections.description: All Cyan API requests require an API key sent in the Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.

content.text: API keys are prefixed with cyan_live_ and are generated from your dashboard. Each key is tied to your account plan and quota.
Your API key is shown only once at the time of creation. Store it securely in your environment variables. Cyan does not store the raw key and cannot recover it.
How to get an API key

items: Log in to the Cyan dashboard
Click Generate New Key, provide a name and optional expiry
Copy the key - it will not be shown again

sections.label: Quickstart
sections.description: The fastest way to test the API. This example converts text to audio in a single request.

content.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/user/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "My First Key"}'

curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/speak \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"text": "Welcome to Cyan. Your AI voice layer.", "provider": "google", "languageCode": "en-US"}'

content.text: Decode the audio field from base64 and save as .mp3 - that is it.

sections.label: Core Concepts
sections.description: Understanding plans, quotas, key types, and request tracing.

content.text: Every API key inherits its owner account plan. Quotas are counted in characters for TTS and seconds for STT. When a quota is exhausted, requests return 429 Too Many Requests with code character_limit_exceeded.
Every request receives a X-Request-ID header in the response. If you provide your own X-Request-ID in the request, Cyan will echo it back. Use this for tracing and debugging.

sections.label: Key Management
sections.description: These endpoints require a JWT (first-party auth). API keys themselves cannot be used to create other API keys.

endpoints.label: Generate API Key
endpoints.description: Creates a new API key for your account. The plaintext key is returned once only.

fields.description: Expiry as RFC3339 timestamp. Omit for no expiry.

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/user/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Production Backend", "expires_at": "2027-01-01T00:00:00Z"}'

responseSchema.description: Key identifier - use this to revoke
The full API key. Shown once only.
Safe prefix for display in UI

endpoints.label: List API Keys
endpoints.description: Returns all keys for your account. Key hashes are never returned.

responseSchema.description: Display prefix (not usable as a key)
Last usage time or null if never used

endpoints.label: Revoke API Key
endpoints.description: Permanently deactivates a key. Cannot be undone.

pathParams.description: ID of the key to revoke

errors.description: Key ID not found or belongs to another account

sections.label: Text-to-Speech (TTS)
sections.description: Convert text to high-quality speech audio. Supports multiple providers, voice cloning, streaming, and chunked delivery. All TTS endpoints accept both a JWT and a direct API key.

endpoints.label: Speak - Base64 Response
endpoints.description: The standard TTS endpoint. Returns the full audio as a base64-encoded string in a JSON response. Best for short texts and situations where you need the full audio before playback.

fields.description: BCP-47 code e.g. en-US. Default: en-US
Device ID for voice clone lookup

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/speak \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "text": "The quarterly earnings report has been finalized.",
    "provider": "elevenlabs",
    "voiceId": "21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM",
    "languageCode": "en-US",
    "sampleRate": 44100
  }'

endpoints.label: Speak - Streaming
endpoints.description: Streams audio bytes as they are generated. Time-to-first-byte is typically under 400ms. Best for real-time playback in applications.

fields.description: BCP-47 code e.g. en-US. Default: en-US
Device ID for voice clone lookup

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/speak-stream \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --output output.mp3 \
  -d '{"text": "Streaming audio in real time with sub-second latency.", "provider": "google", "voiceId": "en-US-Neural2-F", "languageCode": "en-US"}'

responses.code:
Content-Type: audio/mpeg
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Server-Timing: ttfb;dur=312
responses.note: Raw audio bytes are streamed. The Server-Timing: ttfb;dur=<ms> header reports time to first audio byte in milliseconds.

endpoints.label: Speak - Chunked JSON
endpoints.description: Returns audio as an array of base64-encoded chunks. Useful for applications that want to decode and play audio progressively while tracking progress.

fields.description: BCP-47 code e.g. en-US. Default: en-US
Device ID for voice clone lookup

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/speak-chunked \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"text": "This response comes back as a JSON array of audio chunks.", "provider": "google", "languageCode": "en-US"}'

responseSchema.description: Ordered base64 audio segments - concatenate to reconstruct full audio

endpoints.label: Clone Voice and Speak
endpoints.description: Clone a voice from an audio sample, then synthesize text with that cloned voice. Returns the full audio buffer.

fields.description: Text to synthesize with the cloned voice
Device ID to bind the cloned voice to
Audio sample for voice cloning (WAV or MP3, 10s-3min recommended)

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/clone-and-speak \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -F "text=Hello, this is my cloned voice speaking." \
  -F "device_id=dev-9b2e6a1c-33d4" \
  -F "sample=@voice_sample.wav"

responses.note: Raw audio bytes returned directly. Save to a .mp3 file.

endpoints.label: Clone Voice and Stream
endpoints.description: Same as Clone and Speak but streams the synthesized audio response.

fields.description: Text to synthesize with the cloned voice
Device ID to bind the cloned voice to
Audio sample for voice cloning (WAV or MP3, 10s-3min recommended)

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/tts/clone-and-stream \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -F "text=Streaming my cloned voice now." \
  -F "device_id=dev-9b2e6a1c-33d4" \
  -F "sample=@voice_sample.wav"

endpoints.label: Ocean TTS Endpoints
endpoints.description: The Ocean variants mirror the standard TTS endpoints but route through Cyan managed Ocean service context. Use these when integrating with Ocean-aware workflows.

oceanTable.note: All Ocean endpoints use the same request/response schemas as their standard counterparts.

endpoints.label: Voices Reference
endpoints.description: Common voice IDs for each provider.

sections.label: Speech-to-Text (STT)
sections.description: Convert audio files or streams to text transcripts. Supports JSON (base64 audio) and multipart file upload modes.

endpoints.label: Recognize
endpoints.description: Transcribes audio to text. Accepts either a JSON body with base64-encoded audio or a multipart form with a file upload.

modes.label: Mode A - JSON with base64 audio

exampleRequest.code:
curl -X POST https://api.cyan-os.cc/api/v1/stt/recognize \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cyan_live_a3f9c28e1d047b6f..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"content": "UklGRiQAAABXQVZFZm10IBAAAA...(base64 audio)...", "language": "en-US", "sample_rate": 16000}'

fields.description: Audio file - WAV, MP3, FLAC, OGG

responses.code:
{
  "transcript": "Good morning everyone. Today we will be reviewing the Q3 roadmap and discussing priorities for the next sprint.",
  "language": "en-US",
  "chars": 109,
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "totalMs": 1842
}

sections.label: Summarization
sections.description: Generate AI-powered summaries from session transcripts. Ideal for meeting notes, call summaries, and conversation insights. All summarization endpoints accept both a JWT and a direct API key.

endpoints.label: Generate Summary
endpoints.description: Submit an array of session transcripts and receive a structured AI summary.

fields.description: Array of transcript entries from the session
Additional context for the summarization model

responses.code:
{
  "sessionId": "sess_7f3a9b2c-1d4e-5f6a-8b7c-9d0e1f2a3b4c",
  "summary": "The team conducted a sprint review. Out of 21 planned story points, 18 were completed.",
  "summary_title": "Sprint Review - July 11",
  "keyTopics": ["sprint review", "story points", "backlog carryover"],
  "sentiment": "neutral",
  "status": "completed",
  "turn_count": 3,
  "validation_passed": true,
  "metadata": { "sourceLang": "en", "targetLang": "fr", "processedAt": "2026-07-11T08:00:45Z" },
  "summary_with_sources": {
    "summary": "The team completed 18/21 story points in the sprint.",
    "key_points": ["18 of 21 story points completed", "3 tasks carried over to next sprint"],
    "main_topics": ["sprint velocity", "backlog management"],
    "participants": ["Speaker A", "Speaker B"]
  }
}

responseSchema.description: Whether the response passed quality checks
Detailed breakdown with key points and participants

endpoints.label: Get Summary
endpoints.description: Retrieve a previously generated summary by session ID.

pathParams.description: Session ID used in the summarization request

responses.code:
{
  "id": 42,
  "session_id": "sess_7f3a9b2c-1d4e-5f6a-8b7c-9d0e1f2a3b4c",
  "user_id": "9b2e6a1c-33d4-4f7a-b812-1e5a9c2d3f0e",
  "summary": "The team conducted a sprint review. Out of 21 planned story points, 18 were completed.",
  "summary_title": "Sprint Review - July 11",
  "metadata": { "sourceLang": "en", "targetLang": "fr" },
  "created_at": "2026-07-11T08:00:45Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-07-11T08:00:45Z"
}

endpoints.label: List Summaries
endpoints.description: Paginated list of all summaries for the authenticated account.

responseSchema.description: Total number of summaries for this account

sections.label: Error Handling
sections.description: All error responses follow a consistent JSON structure.

content.label: Common error codes
rows: API key or JWT is missing, invalid, or expired
Request body is malformed or missing required fields
Text exceeds the provider character limit per request
ElevenLabs account has no remaining credits
PCM streaming not available for this provider
API key does not exist or has been revoked
API key has passed its expiry date

sections.label: Rate Limits
sections.description: Rate limits are applied per user account based on plan tier, using a sliding window.

content.text: When rate limited, the API returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.

items: Cache GET /user/api-keys and GET /summarization/list responses where appropriate
Use streaming (/tts/speak-stream) for long texts to reduce perceived latency
